summary: Iteration 6 backend regression — 18 pytest cases covering password-reset, invites, digest, AI reconcile cache, subscription regression, EPRA, sync isolation, catch-all and audit entries. 17/18 passed. Single failure is a real backend bug: POST /api/digest/send does NOT insert a 'digest.send' row in audit_log although the review request mandates it (audit log contract violation).

minor.issue: Missing audit_log insert. The endpoint calls services.digest.send_digest_to_user(db, user) which persists to daily_digests but never writes any row to db.audit_log. Spec says 'Audit log: digest.send and ai.reconcile_mpesa actions create rows in audit_log'. ai.reconcile_mpesa IS recorded; digest.send is NOT. Fix: insert {action: 'digest.send', user_id, at, meta:{date, delivery}} after send_digest_to_user returns. Reproduced: POST /api/digest/send for fresh user, then GET /api/audit-log -> only 'user.register' present, no 'digest.send'. Priority: MEDIUM (digest works, history persisted, only audit trail missing).

action_items: Add audit_log insert in POST /api/digest/send (or inside services.digest.send_digest_to_user) so that the digest.send action is auditable, matching the contract verified for ai.reconcile_mpesa.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1298 lines — split into routers (auth.py, invites.py, digest.py, ai.py, payments_stripe.py, mpesa.py, sync.py) for maintainability.

services/digest.send_digest_to_user does NOT write audit_log; inconsistent with ai_reconcile and password_reset_confirm which both audit-log. Add 'digest.send' audit entry alongside the daily_digests upsert.

Password-reset request returns delivery={skipped:'no_key'} as expected, but the response body also includes email_sent:false and the full delivery dict — fine for testing, but in production this leaks delivery infra status. Consider gating delivery field behind an admin/debug flag.

ai_reconcile_cache key is SHA256 over (receipt, paidIn) for inflows and (id, amount|total) for sales — date/sale-fuel fields are NOT included, so two different reconciliation requests that happen to share the same id+amount tuples will collide. Add date and fuel to the cache key payload.

Invite accept allows an existing user (any email) to have their role silently re-assigned via /api/invites/accept (no password check, no auth check) — once an invite code is leaked, anyone holding it can downgrade/upgrade an existing user's role. Add: if existing user, require valid auth or password verification before role change; or reject the invite when the email already belongs to a registered user.
POST /api/auth/password-reset/request is not rate-limited; an attacker can enumerate emails via timing or spam reset attempts. Add a per-IP/per-email rate limiter and a fixed-delay response.
POST /api/invites does not enforce uniqueness — same email can have multiple pending invites; create_invite should upsert / reject if a pending invite already exists for that email.

/api/digest/preview and /api/digest/send call build_digest_for_user which always uses _yesterday_iso() — UI cannot request a digest for an arbitrary date for back-fill testing. Add optional ?date= query param.
Catch-all stub still returns ok:true,stub:true for any unknown /api path — masks typo'd client routes in production. Suggest disabling stub when NODE_ENV/APP_ENV is production.

success_rate.backend: 94% (17/18 passed; 1 functional gap in audit logging)

test_credentials: Per-run fresh users TEST_<uuid>@fuelpro.app / password123. Password reset code retrieved directly from MongoDB (db.password_resets) via pymongo at mongodb://localhost:27017/fuelpro.
seed_data_creation: Created per-test users: owner (user_a fixture), owner (user_b fixture), 1 password-reset user, 1 invitee, 1 staff (via invite accept), 1 ai_reconcile_cache row, 1 daily_digests row, 1 sync_sales item. Existing TEST_*@fuelpro.app records from prior iterations not cleaned up — recommend periodic Mongo prune.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two regression suites now exist: (1) /app/backend/tests/test_fuelpro_backend.py (iteration 3 baseline — Stripe/M-PESA/sync) and (2) /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6_features.py (this iteration — auth+invites+digest+AI cache+audit). Run both. The Stripe status xfail from iteration_1.json is still open. The new failure (digest.send missing audit row) is the only iteration-6 regression. Password reset request deliberately suppresses the code from the HTTP response, so the test reads it via pymongo MongoClient('mongodb://localhost:27017').fuelpro.password_resets.find_one({email:...}) — keep that pattern for future iterations. AI reconcile cache verified: first call cached=false, second identical body returns cached=true with identical matches (SHA256 cache key working). Notifications correctly fall back to skipped:'no_key' for Resend/Twilio when keys are blank.
rca of the issue:
BUG: /api/digest/send missing audit row. RCA: services/digest.py send_digest_to_user() upserts into daily_digests collection but never calls db.audit_log.insert_one. Compare with ai_reconcile (server.py line 1107-1113) which DOES audit. Reproduction: register user -> POST /api/digest/send (token) -> GET /api/audit-log -> response only contains 'user.register'. Mitigation: append the following after the daily_digests.update_one upsert in send_digest_to_user (services/digest.py ~line 175):

    await db.audit_log.insert_one({
        'id': str(uuid.uuid4()),
        'user_id': user['id'],
        'action': 'digest.send',
        'at': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        'meta': {'date': d['date'], 'delivery_ok': delivery.get('ok', False), 'matched': d.get('matched', 0)},
    })

Alternatively, write the audit row in the FastAPI route handler /api/digest/send so all auditing logic lives in server.py.
